architecture.summary: LayoutLLM-T2I is a two-stage text-to-image generation system. Stage 1 uses an LLM with in-context learning to generate a coarse-grained spatial layout (bounding boxes and object positions) from a text prompt, without requiring manual guidance. Stage 2 conditions a diffusion model (building on Stable Diffusion) on both the original text prompt and the automatically generated layout to produce high-fidelity images via an object-interaction diffusion method. Information flows from text → LLM → layout representation → diffusion model → image.

biblio.title: LayoutLLM-T2I: Eliciting Layout Guidance from LLM for Text-to-Image Generation

claims.statement: Current text-to-image models face misalignment issues including problematic spatial relation understanding and numeration failure in complex natural scenes
Proposes a coarse-to-fine paradigm to achieve layout planning and image generation without requiring manual guidance information from users
Generates coarse-grained layout conditioned on textual prompt via in-context learning based on Large Language Models
Proposes a fine-grained object-interaction diffusion method to synthesize high-faithfulness images conditioned on the prompt and automatically generated layout
Method outperforms state-of-the-art models in terms of layout and image generation
